question_paper_html:
Compartment Exam - 2025

Code: 65/S/3 (set-3)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  3. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book at the given place before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This Question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Question paper is divided into FIVE Sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Question Number 1 to 18 are Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and Question Number {tex}19 \& 20{/tex} are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Question Number 21 to 25 are Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Question Number 26 to 31 are Short Answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Question Number 32 to 35 are Long Answer (LA) type questions, carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Question Number 36 to 38 are case study based questions, carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section - B, 3 questions in Section - C, 2 questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{D}{/tex} and {tex}{2}{/tex} questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{E}{/tex}.
  9. Use of calculator is NOT allowed.

SECTION - A

This section comprises multiple choice questions (MCQs) of 1 mark each.
1.

The principal value of cos-1 {tex}\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex} is

a. {tex}\frac{-\pi}{4}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{3\pi}{4}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{5\pi}{4}{/tex}
1
2.

The matrix {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 4\end{array}\right]{/tex} is:

a. an identity matrix
b. a scalar matrix
c. a symmetric matrix
d. a skew-symmetric matrix
1
3.

If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}3 {x} & 5\end{array}\right] \cdot\left[\begin{array}{c}{x} \\ -15\end{array}\right]=0{/tex}, then the value of x is:

a. zero
b. 5
c. {tex}2 \sqrt{5}{/tex}
d. {tex}\pm 5{/tex}
1
4.

If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ll}1 & 2 \\ 4 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}|3 {~A}|={k}|{A}|{/tex}, then the value of k is:

a. 3
b. 9
c. 6
d. 27
1
5.

The area of a triangle with vertices {tex}(4,-1),(1, {k}){/tex} and ({tex}-2,-1{/tex}) is 9 sq units. The value of {tex}k{/tex} is:

a. 8
b. -8
c. 2
d. 6
1
6.

If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 1 & 4\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then the value of {tex}|\operatorname{adj} A|{/tex} is:

a. 5
b. 25
c. -5
d. -25
1
7.

If {tex}\frac{d}{d x} f(x)=3 x^{2}-\frac{3}{x^{4}}{/tex} such that {tex}f(1)=0{/tex}, then {tex}f(x){/tex} is:

a. {tex}6 {x}+\frac{12}{{x}^{5}}{/tex}
b. {tex}{x}^{4}-\frac{1}{{x}^{3}}+2{/tex}
c. {tex}{x}^{3}+\frac{1}{{x}^{3}}-2{/tex}
d. {tex}{x}^{3}+\frac{1}{{x}^{3}}+2{/tex}
1
8.

If {tex}{x}=\sin {t}{/tex} and {tex}{y}=\cos {t}{/tex}, then {tex}\frac{{d}^{2} {y}}{{dx}^{2}}{/tex} at {tex}{t}=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} is:

a. -1
b. 2
c. {tex}2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
d. {tex}-2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
1
9.

The area bounded by the line {tex}y=x, y{/tex}-axis and the lines {tex}y=0{/tex} and {tex}y=4{/tex} is:

a. 16 sq. units
b. 8 sq. units
c. 4 sq. units
d. 2 sq. units
1
10.

The maximum value of {tex}Z=3 x+4 y{/tex} subject to the constraints {tex}x+y \leq 1{/tex}, {tex}{x}, {y} \geq 0{/tex} is:

a. 3
b. 4
c. 7
d. 0
1
11.

In an LPP, corner points of the feasible region determined by the system of linear constraints are {tex}(1,1),(3,0){/tex} and {tex}(0,3){/tex}. If {tex}{Z}={ax}+{by}{/tex}, where {tex}a, b \gt 0{/tex} is to be minimized, the condition on {tex}a{/tex} and {tex}b{/tex}, so that the minimum of Z occurs at (3,0) and (1,1), will be:

a. {tex}a=2 b{/tex}
b. {tex}a=\frac{b}{2}{/tex}
c. {tex}a=3 b{/tex}
d. {tex}a=b{/tex}
1
12.

If {tex}|\vec{a}|=1,|\vec{b}|=2{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=2{/tex}, then the value of {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|{/tex} is:

a. 9
b. 3
c. -3
d. 2
1
13.

{tex}\int \frac{\cot \sqrt{{x}}}{\sqrt{{x}}} {dx}{/tex} is equal to:

a. {tex}\log \sin \sqrt{{x}}+{C}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{\log \sin \sqrt{{x}}}{2 \sqrt{{x}}}+{C}{/tex}
c. {tex}2 \log \sin \sqrt{{x}}+{C}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{\log \sin \sqrt{{x}}}{\sqrt{{x}}}+{C}{/tex}
1
14.

If the rate of change of volume of a sphere is twice the rate of change of its radius, then the surface area of the sphere is:

a. 1 sq. unit
b. 2 sq. units
c. 3 sq. units
d. 4 sq. units
1
15.

If '{tex}m{/tex}' and '{tex}n{/tex}' are the degree and order respectively of the differential equation {tex}1+\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{3}=\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex}, then the value of {tex}(m+n){/tex} is:

a. 4
b. 3
c. 2
d. 5
1
16.

A coin is tossed three times. The probability of getting at least two heads is:

a. {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{3}{8}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{1}{8}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
1
17.

Two vectors {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are such that {tex}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex}. The angle between the two vectors is:

a. {tex}30^{\circ}{/tex}
b. {tex}60^{\circ}{/tex}
c. {tex}45^{\circ}{/tex}
d. {tex}90^{\circ}{/tex}
1
18.

The integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{x}{e^{x}}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}
c. xex
d. ex
1

Questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion and Reason based questions. Two statements are given, one labelled Assertion (A) and the other labelled Reason (R). Select the correct answer from the codes (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below.

  1. (A) Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. (B) Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. (C) Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. (D) Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true. 
19.

Assertion (A): {tex}{f}({x})=[{x}], {x} \in \mathbb{R}{/tex}, the greatest integer function is not differentiable at {tex}x=2{/tex}.
Reason {tex}(R){/tex}: The greatest integer function is not continuous at any integral value.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
20.

Consider the function f : R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R defined as f(x) = x3
Assertion (A): f(x) is a one-one function.
Reason (R): f(x) is a one-one function if co-domain = range.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

SECTION B

This section comprises very short answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each
21.

If {tex}e^{y}(x+1)=1{/tex}, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-e^{y}{/tex}.

 2
22.(i)

Find the principal value of {tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)+2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex}.

 2

OR
(ii)

Prove that:
{tex}\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{{x}}=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-{x}}{1+{x}}\right), {x} \in[0,1]{/tex}

 2
23.

A spherical balloon has a variable diameter {tex}\frac{2}{3}(2 t+1){/tex}. Find the rate of change of its volume with respect to {tex}t{/tex}.

 2
24.

Find the angle between the lines
{tex}\vec{r}=(3+2 \lambda) \hat{i}-(2-2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(6+2 \lambda) \hat{k} \text { and }{/tex}
{tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{{j}}-5 \hat{{k}})+\mu(6 \hat{{i}}+3 \hat{{j}}+2 \hat{{k}}){/tex}.

 2
25.(i)

Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, if the points {tex}(-1,-1,2),(2,8, \lambda){/tex} and {tex}(3,11,6){/tex} are collinear.

 2

OR
(ii)

{tex}\vec{{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{{b}}{/tex} are two co-initial vectors forming the adjacent sides of a parallelogram such that {tex}|\vec{a}|=10,|\vec{b}|=2{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=12{/tex}. Find the area of the parallelogram.

 2

SECTION C

This section comprises short answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.

Find the intervals on which the function f(x) = -2x3 - 9x2 - 12x + 1 is

  1. strictly increasing
  2. strictly decreasing.
 3
27.(i)

Find:
{tex}\int \sqrt{4 x^{2}-4 x+10} d x{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate:
{tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \sin x}{1+\cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex}

 3
28.

If {tex}\hat{a}, \hat{b}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{c}{/tex} are unit vectors such that {tex}\hat{a} \cdot \hat{b}=\hat{a} \cdot \hat{c}=0{/tex} and the angle between {tex}\hat{b}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{c}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}, then prove that {tex}\hat{a}= \pm 2(\hat{b} \times \hat{c}){/tex}.

 3
29.

Solve the following LPP graphically:
Maximize {tex}{Z}=2 {x}+3 {y}{/tex}
subject to the constraints {tex}x+4 y \leq 8{/tex}
{tex}2 x+3 y \leq 12{/tex}
{tex}3 x+y \leq 9{/tex}
{tex}x \geq 0, y \geq 0{/tex}

 3
30.(i)

Four students of class XII are given a problem to solve independently. Their chances of solving the problem respectively are {tex}\frac{1}{2}, \frac{1}{3}, \frac{2}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}. Find the probability that at most one of them will solve the problem.

 3

OR
(ii)

The probability distribution of a random variable X is given below:

X 1 2 4 2 k 3 k 5 k
{tex}{P}({X}){/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{25}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{10}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{25}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{25}{/tex}

Find k, if {tex}{E}({X})=2 \cdot 94{/tex} and also find {tex}{P}({X} \leq 4){/tex}.

 3
31.(i)

Find the general solution of the differential equation
{tex}\left(2 x^{2}+y\right) d x=x d y .{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}-\frac{{y}}{{x}}+\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{{y}}{{x}}\right)=0{/tex}; given that {tex}{y}=0{/tex}, when {tex}{x}=1{/tex}.

 3

SECTION D

This section comprises long answer (LA) type questions of 5 marks each.
32.

If {tex}y=\cos x^{2}+\cos ^{2} x+\cos ^{2}\left(x^{2}\right)+\cos \left(x^{x}\right){/tex}, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.

 5
33.

Find the intervals in which the function given by f(x) = {tex}\frac { 3 } { 10 } x ^ { 4 } - \frac { 4 } { 5 } x ^ { 3 } - 3 x ^ { 2 } + \frac { 36 x } { 5 } + 11{/tex} is

  1. strictly increasing
  2. strictly decreasing
 5
33.

If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{B}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find BA and use this to solve the system of equations:
{tex}{y}+2 {z}=8{/tex},
{tex}x-y=-1{/tex},
{tex}2 {x}+3 {y}+4 {z}=20{/tex}

 5
34.(i)

Find the shortest distance between the lines {tex}l_{1}{/tex} and {tex}l_{2}{/tex} given by:
{tex}l_{1}: \vec{{r}} =\hat{{i}}+2 \hat{{j}}-4 \hat{{k}}+\lambda(4 \hat{{i}}+6 \hat{{j}}+12 \hat{{k}}){/tex} {tex}\text { and } l_{2}: \vec{{r}} =3 \hat{{i}}+3 \hat{{j}}-5 \hat{{k}}+\mu(6 \hat{{i}}+9 \hat{{j}}+18 \hat{{k}}){/tex}

 5

OR
(ii)

Show that the lines {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}=z{/tex} intersect. Also, find their point of intersection.

 5
35.

Using integration, find the area of the region
{tex}\left\{(x, y): 0 \leq y \leq x^{2}, 0 \leq y \leq x, 0 \leq x \leq 3\right\} .{/tex}

 5

SECTION E

This section comprises 3 case study-based questions of 4 marks each.

Case Study - 1
36.

A window is in the form of a rectangle surmounted by an equilateral triangle on its length. Let the rectangular part have length and breadth x and y metres respectively.
Based on the given information, answer the following questions:

  1. If the perimeter of the window is 12 m, find the relation between x and y. (1)
  2. Using the expression obtained in(i), write an expression for the area of the window as a function of {tex}x{/tex} only. (1)
    1. Find the dimensions of the rectangle that will allow maximum light through the window. (use expression obtained in(ii)) (2)

      OR

    2. If it is given that the area of the window is 50 m2, find an expression for its perimeter in terms of x. (2)
 4

Case Study - 2
37.

During the festival season, there was a mela organized by the Resident Welfare Association at a park, near the society. The main attraction of the mela was a huge swing installed at one corner of the park. The swing is traced to follow the path of a parabola given by {tex}x^{2}=y{/tex}.
Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Let {tex}{f}: {N} \longrightarrow {R}{/tex} is defined by {tex}{f}({x})={x}^{2}{/tex} . What will be the range? (1)
  2. Let {tex}{f}: {N} \longrightarrow {N}{/tex} is defined by {tex}{f}({x})={x}^{2}{/tex} . Check if the function is injective or not. (1)
    1. Let {tex}{f}:\{1,2,3,4 \ldots ..\} \longrightarrow\{1,4,9,16 \ldots .{/tex}{tex}\text {be defined by f(x)} = x^2{/tex}. Prove that the function is bijective. (2)

      OR

    2. Let {tex}f: \mathbb{R} \longrightarrow \mathbb{R}{/tex} is defined by {tex}f(x)=x^{2}{/tex} . Show that {tex}f{/tex} is neither injective nor surjective. (2)
 4

Case Study - 3
38.

Two persons are competing for a position on the Managing Committee of an organisation. The probabilities that the first and the second person will be appointed are 0.5 and 0.6 respectively. Also, if the first person gets appointed, then the probability of introducing waste treatment plant is {tex}0. 7{/tex} and the corresponding probability is {tex}0.4{/tex}, if the second person gets appointed.
Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. What is the probability that the waste treatment plant is introduced?
  2. After the selection, if the waste treatment plant is introduced, what is the probability that the first person had introduced it?
 4

The integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{x}{e^{x}}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}
c. xex
d. ex
Correct Answer: (b) {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}
Explanation:

We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+y=\frac{1+y}{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x}+\frac{y(1-x)}{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}-\left(\frac{1-x}{x}\right) y=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} 
This is a linear differential equation.
On comparing it with {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex}, we get
{tex}P=\frac{-(1-x)}{x}, Q=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} 
I.F. = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{-\int \frac{1-x}{x} d x}{/tex} 
{tex}=e^{x-\log x}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{x}{/tex}

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

Hence R is false.
Let f (x1) = f(x2) for some x1, x2 {tex}\in{/tex} R

Hence f(x) is one-one.

21.

If {tex}e^{y}(x+1)=1{/tex}, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-e^{y}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}e^{y}(x+1)=1 \Rightarrow e^{y}=\frac{1}{x+1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y=-\log (x+1){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{1}{x+1}{/tex}
{tex}=-e^{y} \left[\because \frac{1}{x+1}=e^{y}\right]{/tex}

22.(i)

Find the principal value of {tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)+2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)+2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\left(\pi-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+2\left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\pi{/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

Prove that:
{tex}\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{{x}}=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-{x}}{1+{x}}\right), {x} \in[0,1]{/tex}

Solution:

Put {tex}x=\tan ^{2} \theta \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x}{/tex}
RHS {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-\tan ^{2} \theta}{1+\tan ^{2} \theta}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1}(\cos 2 \theta){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}(2 \theta){/tex}
{tex}=\theta=\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x}={/tex} LHS

 2
23.

A spherical balloon has a variable diameter {tex}\frac{2}{3}(2 t+1){/tex}. Find the rate of change of its volume with respect to {tex}t{/tex}.

Solution:

Clearly radius {tex}=\frac{2 t+1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}{V}=\frac{4 \pi}{3} \frac{(2 t+1)^{3}}{27}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d v}{d t}=\frac{8 \pi}{27}(2 t+1)^{2}{/tex}

Find the angle between the lines
{tex}\vec{r}=(3+2 \lambda) \hat{i}-(2-2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(6+2 \lambda) \hat{k} \text { and }{/tex}
{tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{{j}}-5 \hat{{k}})+\mu(6 \hat{{i}}+3 \hat{{j}}+2 \hat{{k}}){/tex}.

Solution:

Given lines are: {tex}\vec{r}=(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k})+\lambda(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
and {tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k})+\mu(6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
Let {tex}\theta{/tex} be the angle between these two lines.
{tex}\cos \theta=\frac{2(6)+2(3)+2(2)}{\sqrt{4+4+4} \sqrt{36+9+4}}=\frac{22}{2 \sqrt{3} \times 7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{11}{21} \sqrt{3} \Rightarrow \theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{11}{21} \sqrt{3}\right){/tex}

 2
25.(i)

Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, if the points {tex}(-1,-1,2),(2,8, \lambda){/tex} and {tex}(3,11,6){/tex} are collinear.

Solution:

{tex}A(-1,-1,2), B(2,8, \lambda), C(3,11,6){/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=3 \hat{\imath}+9 \hat{\jmath}+(\lambda-2) \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{B C}=\hat{\imath}+3 \hat{\jmath}+(6-\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex}
Since {tex}A, B{/tex} and {tex}C{/tex} are collinear, {tex}\frac{3}{1}=\frac{9}{3}=\frac{\lambda-2}{6-\lambda}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda=5{/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

{tex}\vec{{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{{b}}{/tex} are two co-initial vectors forming the adjacent sides of a parallelogram such that {tex}|\vec{a}|=10,|\vec{b}|=2{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=12{/tex}. Find the area of the parallelogram.

Solution:

Let {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}.
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=12 \Rightarrow|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \theta=12{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(10)(2) \cos \theta=12 \Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \sin \theta=\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^{2}}=\frac{4}{5}{/tex}
Now, area of parallelogram {tex}=|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \sin \theta{/tex}
{tex}=(10)(2)\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)=16{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} area of parallelogram {tex}=16{/tex}

Find the intervals on which the function f(x) = -2x3 - 9x2 - 12x + 1 is

Here,it is given the function

= -6(x + 2)(x + 1) ... (i)

{tex}\left(2 x^{2}+y\right) d x=x d y .{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\left(2 x^{2}+y\right) d x=x d y{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{1}{x} \cdot y=2 x{/tex}
{tex}\text { I.F. }=e^{-\int \frac{1}{x} d x}=\frac{1}{x}{/tex}

{tex}y \cdot\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\int 2 x \cdot \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec} \frac{y}{x}{/tex}
Put {tex}{y}={v x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}={v}+{x} \frac{{dv}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {v}+{x} \frac{{d v}}{{d x}}={v}-\operatorname{cosec} {v}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-\int \sin {v} {d} {v}=\int \frac{{d} {x}}{{x}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos v=\log |x|+c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \frac{y}{x}=\log |x|+c{/tex}
{tex}{x}={1}, {y}={0} \Rightarrow {c}={1}{/tex}

Solution:

Given lines are: {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k})+2 \lambda(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}
and {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k})+3 \mu(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}
Clearly, the given lines are parallel.
Here, {tex}\vec{a}_{1}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}, \vec{a}_{2}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}\right) \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 2 & 3 & 6\end{array}\right|=9 \hat{i}-14 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left|\left(\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}\right) \times \vec{b}\right|=\sqrt{81+196+16}=\sqrt{293}{/tex}
Also, {tex}|\vec{b}|=\sqrt{4+9+36}=7{/tex}
S.D. {tex}=\frac{\left|\left(\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}\right) \times \vec{b}\right|}{|\vec{b}|}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{293}}{7}{/tex}

Show that the lines {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}=z{/tex} intersect. Also, find their point of intersection.

Solution:

We have lines
{tex}L_{1}: \frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}=\lambda{/tex}
and {tex}L_{2}: \frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}=z=\mu{/tex}
Any point on the line L1 is {tex}(2 \lambda+1,3 \lambda+2,4 \lambda+3){/tex}
Any point on the line L2 is {tex}(5 \mu+4,2 \mu+1, \mu){/tex}
If lines intersect then there exists a value of {tex}\lambda, \mu{/tex} for which {tex}(2 \lambda+1,3 \lambda+2,4 \lambda+3)=(5 \mu+4,2 \mu+1, \mu){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 \lambda+1=5 \mu+4,3 \lambda+2=2 \mu+1{/tex} and {tex}4 \lambda+3=\mu{/tex}
Solving first two equations we get {tex}\lambda=-1, \mu=-1{/tex}
These values of {tex}\lambda=-1, \mu=-1{/tex} also satisfy the third equation.
Thus lines intersect.
Also the point of intersections is (-1, -1, -1).

Case Study - 3
38.

Two persons are competing for a position on the Managing Committee of an organisation. The probabilities that the first and the second person will be appointed are 0.5 and 0.6 respectively. Also, if the first person gets appointed, then the probability of introducing waste treatment plant is {tex}0. 7{/tex} and the corresponding probability is {tex}0.4{/tex}, if the second person gets appointed.
Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. What is the probability that the waste treatment plant is introduced?
  2. After the selection, if the waste treatment plant is introduced, what is the probability that the first person had introduced it?
Solution:

{tex}E_{1}{/tex}: Event that the first person is appointed.
{tex}E_{2}{/tex}: Event that the second person is appointed.
A: Event that the waste treatment plant is introducted.
Here, {tex}{P}\left(E_{1}\right)=0.5, P\left(E_{2}\right)=0.6{/tex}
{tex}P\left(A \mid E_{1}\right)=0.7, P\left(A \mid E_{2}\right)=0.4{/tex}

  1. {tex}P{/tex} (waste treatment plant is introducted)
    {tex}=P\left(E_{1}\right) P\left(A \mid E_{1}\right)+P\left(E_{2}\right) P\left(A \mid E_{2}\right){/tex}
    {tex}=(0.5)(0.7)+(0.6)(0.4){/tex}
    {tex}=0.35+0.24=0.59{/tex}
  2. {tex} P\left(E_1 \mid A\right) =\frac{P\left(E_1\right) P\left(A \mid E_1\right)}{P\left(E_1\right) P\left(A \mid E_1\right)+P\left(E_2\right) P\left(A \mid E_2\right)} {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{(0.5)(0.7)}{(0.5)(0.7)+(0.6)(0.4)} {/tex}
    {tex}= \frac{0.35}{0.59}=\frac{35}{59}{/tex}

If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}{B}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find BA and use this to solve the system of equations: {tex}{y}+2 {z}=8{/tex} , {tex}x-y=-1{/tex} , {tex}2 {x}+3 {y}+4 {z}=20{/tex} Solution: {tex}{BA}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 6 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 6 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 6 \end{array}\right]=6 {I}{/tex} Given system is {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}-1 \\ 20 \\ 8\end{array}\right]{/tex} i.e., {tex}{BX}={C}{/tex} say so {tex}{X}={B}^{-1} {C}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{6} A C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 2 & -4 \\ -4 & 2 & -4 \\ 2 & -1 & 5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} -1 \\ 20 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}{x}=1, {y}=2, {z}=3{/tex} 5 34. (i) Find the shortest distance between the lines {tex}l_{1}{/tex} and {tex}l_{2}{/tex} given by: {tex}l_{1}: \vec{{r}} =\hat{{i}}+2 \hat{{j}}-4 \hat{{k}}+\lambda(4 \hat{{i}}+6 \hat{{j}}+12 \hat{{k}}){/tex} {tex}\text { and } l_{2}: \vec{{r}} =3 \hat{{i}}+3 \hat{{j}}-5 \hat{{k}}+\mu(6 \hat{{i}}+9 \hat{{j}}+18 \hat{{k}}){/tex} Solution: Given lines are: {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k})+2 \lambda(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k})+3 \mu(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex} Clearly, the given lines are parallel. Here, {tex}\vec{a}_{1}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}, \vec{a}_{2}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\left(\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}\right) \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 2 & 3 & 6\end{array}\right|=9 \hat{i}-14 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\therefore\left|\left(\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}\right) \times \vec{b}\right|=\sqrt{81+196+16}=\sqrt{293}{/tex} Also, {tex}|\vec{b}|=\sqrt{4+9+36}=7{/tex} S.D. {tex}=\frac{\left|\left(\vec{a}_{2}-\vec{a}_{1}\right) \times \vec{b}\right|}{|\vec{b}|}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{293}}{7}{/tex} 5 OR (ii) Show that the lines {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}=z{/tex} intersect. Also, find their point of intersection. Solution: We have lines {tex}L_{1}: \frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}=\lambda{/tex} and {tex}L_{2}: \frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}=z=\mu{/tex} Any point on the line L 1 is {tex}(2 \lambda+1,3 \lambda+2,4 \lambda+3){/tex} Any point on the line L 2 is {tex}(5 \mu+4,2 \mu+1, \mu){/tex} If lines intersect then there exists a value of {tex}\lambda, \mu{/tex} for which {tex}(2 \lambda+1,3 \lambda+2,4 \lambda+3)=(5 \mu+4,2 \mu+1, \mu){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 \lambda+1=5 \mu+4,3 \lambda+2=2 \mu+1{/tex} and {tex}4 \lambda+3=\mu{/tex} Solving first two equations we get {tex}\lambda=-1, \mu=-1{/tex} These values of {tex}\lambda=-1, \mu=-1{/tex} also satisfy the third equation. Thus lines intersect. Also the point of intersections is (-1, -1, -1). 5 35. Using integration, find the area of the region {tex}\left\{(x, y): 0 \leq y \leq x^{2}, 0 \leq y \leq x, 0 \leq x \leq 3\right\} .{/tex} Solution: {tex}\text { Required Area }{/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{1} x^{2} d x+\int_{1}^{3} x d x{/tex} {tex}\left.\left.=\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right]_{0}^{1}+\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{1}^{3}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{3}+4=\frac{13}{3}{/tex} 5 SECTION E This section comprises 3 case study-based questions of 4 marks each.
